title: triticum dicoccum subsp. tricoccum
intro: Triticum dicoccum subsp. tricoccum, also known as wild einkorn or two-hulled einkorn, is a subspecies of the einkorn wheat plant that is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Triticum dicoccum subsp. tricoccum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or flour for use in baking, making it a versatile and nutritious addition to a healthy diet.
